user_message: Give me the manager view on this Quantum Analytics meeting transcript. This is time-sensitive -- customer is waiting.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: forecast prep Discussion - Quantum Analytics
Date: 2024-01-15
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Robert Foster (Quantum Analytics), Marcus Williams (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Robert Foster (Quantum Analytics): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on forecast prep from our last session.

[00:08:00] Robert Foster (Quantum Analytics): On the forecast prep front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

[00:12:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the forecast prep front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around cost optimization.

[00:14:00] Robert Foster (Quantum Analytics): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:17:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:21:00] Robert Foster (Quantum Analytics): The timeline for partner engagement is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF47-I trap (Misses side-conversation commitments); produces complete Decision log with speaker attribution, action distribution by role, stakeholder map delta, MSX updat
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF47-I failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF47-I failure mode (Misses side-conversation commitments)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct forecast prep decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF47-I -- Misses side-conversation commitments
